# Vault Radar correlation (API)

## Overview

The HCP Vault Radar correlation API lets you correlate detected secrets in your
platform with secret manager locations that Vault Radar indexed through the
Vault Radar Agent. Use these endpoints to retrieve a project-specific secret
hasher key, hash detected secrets locally, and look up matching managed
locations.

## Versioning

The Vault Radar API version is `2023-05-01`.

## Prerequisites

Before you call the correlation endpoints, complete the following setup:

- Sign in to HCP Vault Radar and [deploy the Vault Radar agent](https://developer.hashicorp.com/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy).
- Connect a supported secret manager, such as [Vault](https://developer.hashicorp.com/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/correlate-vault) or [AWS Secrets Manager](https://developer.hashicorp.com/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/correlate-aws-secrets-manager), through the agent.
- Create a service principal in the HCP project and assign the `Vault Radar Correlator` role.

  ![Vault Radar Correlator role assignment](https://developer.hashicorp.com/img/docs/vault-radar/correlation_role.png)

- Generate a client ID and client secret for the service principal.
- Store the service principal credentials securely in your platform.

## Integration flow

Use the following flow when you integrate your platform with Vault Radar:

1. Add a **Connect with Vault Radar** action in your platform.
1. Collect the service principal credentials from the user and store them securely.
1. Exchange the credentials for a bearer token.
1. Retrieve the project-specific secret hasher key.
1. Hash each detected secret locally.
1. Send the hashes to the correlate endpoint.
1. Display the managed locations that Vault Radar returns so users can review where the secret is stored.

## Authentication

Use the HCP Auth API to exchange the service principal credentials for a bearer
token. Refer to [HCP Vault Radar API authentication](https://developer.hashicorp.com/hcp/docs/vault-radar/api/authenticate)
for more information.

```shell-session
$ curl --location "https://auth.idp.hashicorp.com/oauth2/token" \
  --header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  --data-urlencode "client_id=$HCP_CLIENT_ID" \
  --data-urlencode "client_secret=$HCP_CLIENT_SECRET" \
  --data-urlencode "grant_type=client_credentials" \
  --data-urlencode "audience=https://api.hashicorp.cloud"
```

The response uses the following format:

```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR...",
  "expires_in": 3600,
  "token_type": "Bearer"
}
```

## Get a secret hasher key

`GET` `/2023-05-01/vault-radar/projects/{project_id}/api/public/secret-hasher-key`

Retrieve the project-specific secret hasher key that you use as the Argon2id
salt input when you hash detected secrets locally.

```plaintext
https://api.cloud.hashicorp.com/2023-05-01/vault-radar/projects/{project_id}/api/public/secret-hasher-key
```

### Request

#### Path parameters

`project_id` (string, required): The unique identifier of the HCP project where
you enabled Vault Radar. You can find the project ID in the URL of your browser
or in the project settings in the HCP Portal.

#### Example request

```shell-session
$ curl --location "https://api.cloud.hashicorp.com/2023-05-01/vault-radar/projects/$HCP_PROJECT_ID/api/public/secret-hasher-key" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $HCP_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  --header "Content-Type: application/json"
```

### Response

`200 - OK`

```json
{
  "secret_hasher_key": "<Base64-encoded 16-byte key without padding>"
}
```

## Hashing requirements

Hash each detected secret locally before you send it to the correlate endpoint.
Use the `secret_hasher_key` value as the Argon2id salt input.

Use the following Argon2id parameters:

- Memory (`m`): `47104` (`46 MiB`)
- Iterations (`t`): `1`
- Parallelism (`p`): `1`
- Digest length: `32` bytes

After you generate the digest, Base64-encode the hash without padding.

## Correlate hashes

`POST` `/2023-05-01/vault-radar/projects/{project_id}/api/public/correlate`

Send one or more locally generated hashes to retrieve the managed locations that
Vault Radar indexed for each matching secret.

```plaintext
https://api.cloud.hashicorp.com/2023-05-01/vault-radar/projects/{project_id}/api/public/correlate
```

### Request

#### Path parameters

`project_id` (string, required): The unique identifier of the HCP project where
you enabled Vault Radar. You can find the project ID in the URL of your browser
or in the project settings in the HCP Portal.

#### Example body

```json
{
  "hashes": [
    "<Base64-encoded hash without padding>",
    "<Base64-encoded hash without padding>"
  ]
}
```

#### Example request

```shell-session
$ curl --location "https://api.cloud.hashicorp.com/2023-05-01/vault-radar/projects/$HCP_PROJECT_ID/api/public/correlate" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $HCP_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "hashes": [
      "<Base64-encoded hash without padding>",
      "<Base64-encoded hash without padding>"
    ]
  }'
```

### Response

`200 - OK`

```json
{
  "result": [
    {
      "hash": "Te0QR/JlwFrG1x2MeKEZRCup7pHO/7fg77o8Stzpzcw",
      "managed_locations": [
        {
          "uri": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:eu-north-1:469970950051:secret:test/databricks-gl0MFi:a618a006-5cd9-4a3b-aae2-2f7f80fde35d:key1",
          "type": "aws_secrets_manager",
          "metadata": {
            "aws_account_id": "469970950051",
            "aws_account_name": "sample_account",
            "aws_region": "eu-north-1",
            "description": "KK description of databricks",
            "is_latest": true,
            "secret_name": "test/databricks",
            "tags": {
              "first_secret": "sample"
            },
            "version": "a618a006-5cd9-4a3b-aae2-2f7f80fde35d"
          },
          "created_at": "2025-08-22T08:38:57.430000+00:00"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "hash": "Fl7IXb4xJZIgo8MhJvL3eayFbuoVFVkm9lGWtjbB4yk",
      "managed_locations": [
        {
          "uri": "vault://sample-cluster-public-vault-05647210.5c7fd5fb.z1.hcp.to:8200/admin/secret/data/databricks?key=pat1&version=1",
          "type": "hashicorp_vault",
          "metadata": {
            "custom_metadata": {
              "check_key1": "check_value_1",
              "check_key2": "check_value_2"
            },
            "key": "pat1",
            "mount_path": "secret",
            "mount_type": "kv-v2",
            "namespace": "admin",
            "secret_path": "databricks",
            "version": 1
          },
          "created_at": "2025-08-05T11:43:34.046058+00:00"
        },
        {
          "uri": "vault://sample-cluster-public-vault-05647210.5c7fd5fb.z1.hcp.to:8200/admin/secret/data/databricks?key=pat1&version=2",
          "type": "hashicorp_vault",
          "metadata": {
            "custom_metadata": {
              "check_key1": "check_value_1",
              "check_key2": "check_value_2"
            },
            "key": "pat1",
            "mount_path": "secret",
            "mount_type": "kv-v2",
            "namespace": "admin",
            "secret_path": "databricks",
            "version": 2
          },
          "created_at": "2025-08-05T16:44:10.751479+00:00"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Vault Radar omits unmatched hashes from the response.

## Correlation result fields reference

The correlate endpoint returns a list of matched hashes and the managed
locations for each match.

### Top-level fields

| Field Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `result` | array | List of correlated hashes returned by Vault Radar. |
| `hash` | string | Base64-encoded Argon2id hash for the detected secret. |
| `managed_locations` | array | List of known secret manager locations for the matching secret. |

### Managed location fields

| Field Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `uri` | string | Secret manager URI for the correlated secret. |
| `type` | string | Secret manager type, such as `aws_secrets_manager` or `hashicorp_vault`. |
| `metadata` | json | Provider-specific metadata for the managed secret location. |
| `created_at` | timestamp | Timestamp when Vault Radar recorded the managed location. |

## Integration considerations

When your platform detects a secret, use the correlation API to enrich the
finding with secret manager context.

- Call the correlate endpoint and retrieve the managed locations for the detected secret.
- Display the managed locations in your user interface so users can review where the secret is stored.
- Adjust the finding severity when the managed location changes the security context.
- Keep the service principal credentials in secure storage and issue requests from your backend.
